field_decisions.reason: The product hypothesis is Benelux-facing, but the verified route facts are country-specific and EU-regulated.

field_decisions.value: Nursing is a regulated-profession route. Destination-country recognition or permission-to-practise applies before work can start, and route compliance cannot be collapsed into a generic training offer.
field_decisions.reason: This is the core fact that survived all source checks.

field_decisions.value: Luxembourg requires language proof and a B2 certificate if no other accepted proof is available; Belgium requires sufficient Dutch, French, or German and explicitly says a simple course-attendance certificate is not enough.
field_decisions.reason: This is the most decision-useful route fact for product design.

field_decisions.value: WHO/Europe says the region faces an expected shortage of nearly 1 million health workers by 2030.
field_decisions.reason: Safe macro context only; it should not be treated as local demand proof.
These public analogues disprove empty-market wording and show the category pattern already exists.

value: The legacy exclusivity claim is false; adjacent European models already combine recruitment, language, recognition, and relocation.
The product cannot claim a generic language certificate outcome; Belgium explicitly rejects simple course-attendance proof.
Luxembourg and Belgium route mechanics differ materially and should not be sold as one undifferentiated operational path.
Erasmus+ is only a broad policy funding mention here, not direct proof that this commercial pipeline is eligible.
The £10–17K per nurse cost range is not Benelux-primary-source-safe.
field_decisions.reason: These are the main reasons the card remains blocked for release.

field_decisions.value: Recruitment agencies and hospital employers placing foreign-trained nurses into Belgium and Luxembourg, with nurses as the route participants.
field_decisions.omission_reason: Reasonable synthesis, but still not a default dashboard truth field.
The product does not yet identify a route-recognised exam or certificate path.
Desk research does not prove agencies need or will buy this exact bundled service.
Only a weak Irish analogue was located; Benelux pricing evidence is missing.
Broad policy funding references do not prove this offer fits an actual Erasmus+ application route.
Luxembourg and Belgium language-proof mechanics differ enough that one generic route description is unsafe.
